translation: When the earth is shaken with its earthquake
urdu: جب زمین بھونچال سے ہلا دی جائے گی

translation: And the earth brings out its burdens,
urdu: اور زمین اپنے (اندر) کے بوجھ نکال ڈالے گی

translation: And man wonders, “What is the matter with it?”
urdu: اور انسان کہے گا کہ اس کو کیا ہوا ہے؟

translation: That Day it [the earth] will relate its stories,
urdu: اس روز وہ اپنے حالات بیان کردے گی
کیونکہ تمہارے پروردگار نے اس کو حکم بھیجا (ہوگا)

arabic: يَوۡمَئِذٖ يَصۡدُرُ ٱلنَّاسُ أَشۡتَاتٗا لِّيُرَوۡاْ أَعۡمَٰلَهُمۡ
transliteration: Yawmaithin yasduru alnnasu ashtatan liyuraw aAAmalahum
translation: That Day mankind will proceed in groups to be shown their deeds.
urdu: اس دن لوگ گروہ گروہ ہو کر آئیں گے تاکہ ان کو ان کے اعمال دکھا دیئے جائیں

arabic: فَمَن يَعۡمَلۡ مِثۡقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيۡرٗا يَرَهُۥ
transliteration: Faman yaAAmal mithqala tharratin khayran yarahu
translation: Then whoever does [even] an atom's weight of good deed will see it,
urdu: تو جس نے ذرہ بھر نیکی کی ہو گی وہ اس کو دیکھ لے گا

arabic: وَمَن يَعۡمَلۡ مِثۡقَالَ ذَرَّةٖ شَرّٗا يَرَهُۥ
transliteration: Waman yaAAmal mithqala tharratin sharran yarahu
translation: And whoever does [even] an atom's weight of bad deed will see it.
notes: The subject of this Qur’aanic Chapter is of course the earthquake that would engulf the earth to bring an end to it in its present state; and thereafter how mankind, after Resurrection, would be shown all their deeds, big or small, while they lived on this earth. But there is an astonishing analogy of the description here to an earthquake that occurred in Turkey in 1999. That analogy was mainly shown by a Turkish gentleman soon after the earthquake that year, but a little part of it was discovered by me myself. Please click The Earthquake to read the article thereon.
urdu: اور جس نے ذرہ بھر برائی کی ہوگی وہ اسے دیکھ لے گا
